mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate after a tightening cycle raises the discount rate and tightens financial conditions, which should pressure equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is trending upward for at least 3 months, or rolling 2-year Rank IC stays above 0.03 with the opposite sign, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals weakening household income and demand, which should reduce cyclical earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce non-negative 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the average post-trigger return is positive for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly retail sales growth in RSAFS signals resilient consumer demand and better near-term revenue outlook, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth episodes do not outperform unconditional 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the event-study average is not positive in at least 3 of 4 consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO falls while CPIAUCSL keeps rising, firms face margin compression from weak output and persistent inflation, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined signal does not outperform either proxy alone in out-of-sample tests, or if triggered periods with falling INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL still yield positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below its recent level signals cooling housing demand and weaker household balance-sheet support, which should weigh on broad equity returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-average HOUST periods are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the effect disappears after controlling for broader market trend in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth that remains positive while unemployment stays low indicates durable labor income and consumption support, which should lift earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal does not improve hit rate relative to each series separately, or if low-unemployment payroll-expansion periods fail to produce positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL stops accelerating, markets should infer peak policy tightness and easier future discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate declines
failure_condition: Reject if post-peak-tightening episodes with decelerating CPI do not show positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average forward return is not statistically above zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly improvement in GDPC1 indicates stronger aggregate final demand and better earnings growth prospects, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive on a quarter-over-quarter basis
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal loses significance in any 3-year rolling window.
